title: Transformers: Go!
synopsis: In this Japanese OVA mini-series set in the Transformers: Prime continuity, two teams of ancient Autobot Swordbots awaken to battle the Predacons. When the villainous Dragotron and his four Predacon Oni generals resurface seeking the legendary Legendiscs — mystical artifacts of immense power — the Samurai team (Kenzan, Jinbu, Ganoh) and Shinobi team (Gekisoumaru, Hishoumaru, Sensuimaru) join forces with human allies and Optimus Prime to stop them. Each three-bot team can perform a Triple Combination to form a mighty combined warrior: DaiKenzan for the Samurai and DaiGekisou for the Shinobi.

episodes.title: Triple Combination! Swordbot — Samurai!
episodes.synopsis: The Samurai Swordbots Kenzan, Jinbu, and Ganoh awaken to battle the Predacon threat and perform their first Triple Combination against the Oni generals.

episodes.title: Pursue! The Legendiscs!
episodes.synopsis: The Samurai team pursues the Predacons who are racing to collect the powerful Legendiscs before they can be used for evil.

episodes.title: The Invincible Swordsman — Ryouma Sakamoto!?
episodes.synopsis: The Samurai team encounters a warrior channeling the spirit of the legendary swordsman Ryouma Sakamoto in their quest to protect the Legendiscs.

episodes.title: Showdown! Musashibou Benkei!
episodes.synopsis: A showdown inspired by the legendary warrior monk Musashibou Benkei tests the Samurai team's resolve and combat skills.

episodes.title: Depart! Optimus Exprime!
episodes.synopsis: Optimus Prime arrives in a powerful new Exprime form to aid the Samurai Swordbots in their escalating battle against the Predacons.

episodes.title: Final Battle! Link-Up Triple Combination DaiKenzan!
episodes.synopsis: The Samurai arc reaches its climax as the team performs the ultimate Link-Up Triple Combination to form DaiKenzan and defeat the Predacon threat.

episodes.title: Triple Combination! Swordbot — Ninja!
episodes.synopsis: The Shinobi Swordbots Gekisoumaru, Hishoumaru, and Sensuimaru awaken and engage the Predacon Oni in a parallel battle for the Legendiscs.

episodes.title: Discover! The Legendiscs!
episodes.synopsis: The Shinobi team discovers hidden Legendiscs and must protect them from the Predacon forces hunting the artifacts.

episodes.title: Enter! The New Warrior!
episodes.synopsis: A powerful new warrior joins the Shinobi team's fight, turning the tide against the relentless Predacon onslaught.

episodes.title: Final Battle! Link-Up Triple Combination DaiGekisou!
episodes.synopsis: The Shinobi arc culminates as the team unleashes the Link-Up Triple Combination to form DaiGekisou and vanquish the Predacon menace once and for all.

facts: Transformers: Go! was animated by Tatsunoko Production and distributed via DVDs packaged with Japanese children's magazines TV-Kun (Samurai arc) and TV Magazine (Shinobi arc) starting in 2013.
The series is set in the Transformers: Prime 'Aligned' continuity and features Optimus Prime in a unique 'Exprime' form exclusive to this show.
Opening theme 'TRANSFORMERS-Go!' and ending theme 'Sanjou Gattai! Hero!' were added from the third episode onward in both arcs.
The two arcs ran concurrently — Samurai and Shinobi — each following a different three-bot combiner team fighting the same Predacon threat from different perspectives.
The Swordbots' designs draw heavily from Japanese feudal warrior traditions: the Samurai team embodies bushido honor while the Shinobi team uses stealth and ninja tactics.
Dragotron and the four Oni generals are inspired by Japanese mythology, with 'Oni' being traditional demon figures from Japanese folklore.
